SKL_MP4_ANALYZER Class Reference

#include <skl_mpg4.h>

List of all members.

virtual int Analyze (SKL_MP4_INFOS *const Frame)=0
virtual void Analyze_dQ (SKL_MP4_INFOS *const Frame, int For_BVOP)=0

virtual int Set_Param (const char *const Param, float Value)=0
virtual int Get_Param (const char *const Param, int *Value) const =0
virtual int Get_Param (const char *const Param, float *Value) const =0

virtual SKL_MEM_I * Set_Memory_Manager (SKL_MEM_I *Mem=0)=0
SKL_MEM_I * Get_Mem () const

Detailed Description

Base class for analyzing input, making coding decisions and performing bit-rate control. Actually drives the bitstream-coder (SKL_MP4_ENC). Setting/Access/Control of internal parameter is granted through the Set_Param() and Get_Param() functions. See description of this function for details. We encourage you to have a look at the example 'tmp4.cpp', that exercises every possible params of Set_Param() through the command-line options.

See also:
SKL_MP4_ENC

Member Function Documentation

virtual int SKL_MP4_ANALYZER::Analyze (  SKL_MP4_INFOS *const   Frame  )  [pure virtual]
 

This function is responsible for motion estimation. It should populate the array of motion vectors MVs[] and the macroblock map *Map with the desired macroblock types.

Returns:
return code: 0: intra coding required (I-VOP) 1/2: predictive coding required (1=P or 2=B-VOP) 3: GMC coding requested

virtual void SKL_MP4_ANALYZER::Analyze_dQ (  SKL_MP4_INFOS *const   Frame,
int  For_BVOP
)  [pure virtual]
 

This function handles variable per-macroblocks quantization.

New->Map[].dQ should be filled with dQ hints. This is a separate virtual, not merged with the above Analyze() motion estimation, since the coding type can be changed by the encoder inbetween. This impacts the range of allowed dQ values (eg.: for B-VOP).

SKL_MEM_I* SKL_MP4_ANALYZER::Get_Mem (   )  const [inline]
 

This function returns the current memory pool, as set by Set_Memory_Manager()

See also:
Get_Memory_Manager()

SKL_MEM_I

virtual const int* SKL_MP4_ANALYZER::Get_Param (  const char *const   Param  )  const [pure virtual]
 

Get read-only internal data. Returns NULL is not applicable. This is mainly for internal use.

virtual int SKL_MP4_ANALYZER::Get_Param (  const char *const   Param,
float *  Value
)  const [pure virtual]
 

Returns the current value of the float parameter described by Param string.

Returns:
0 if parameter is not applicable.
See also:
Set_Param()
Parameters:
Param: This string can be one of the corresponding ones passed to Set_Param().
Value pointer the parameter value.

virtual int SKL_MP4_ANALYZER::Get_Param (  const char *const   Param,
int *  Value
)  const [pure virtual]
 

Returns the current value of the integer parameter described by Param string.

Returns:
0 if parameter is not applicable.
See also:
Set_Param()
Parameters:
Value 
Param This string can be one of the corresponding ones passed to Set_Param(). In addition, there are some non-settable parameters, whose values can be accessed using the following string:
  • "fcode": Desired typical length of motion vectors (bitstream syntax).
  • "version": the version of the core (analyzer)
Value pointer the parameter value.

virtual void SKL_MP4_ANALYZER::Post_Coding_Update (  const SKL_MP4_INFOS *const   Infos,
int  Coded_Bits
)  [pure virtual]
 

Post-coding update (for rate control, e.g.)

Parameters:
Infos contains informations about the last coded frame
Coded_Bits is the number of bits used for coding last frame.

virtual SKL_MEM_I* SKL_MP4_ANALYZER::Set_Memory_Manager (  SKL_MEM_I *  Mem = 0  )  [pure virtual]
 

Set a new memory pool to use internally for subsequent memory allocation. Warning: it should preferably be called at start-up only, to ensure that cycles of New()/Delete() are performed on the same memory pool.

Parameters:
Mem The memory pool to use. If equal to 0 (default), the C++ heap will be used.
Returns:
This function will return the previous memory manager.
See also:
SKL_MEM_I

Get_Mem()

virtual int SKL_MP4_ANALYZER::Set_Param (  const char *const   Param,
float  Value
)  [pure virtual]
 

Sets the value of the float parameter described by Param string. These parameter settings control the core of the codec during motion estimation and bitstream coding.

Returns:
0 if parameter is not applicable, or -1 if Value has invalid range.
Parameters:
Param can be one of the following constant strings:
  • "dquant-amp": If non-zero, turns luminance-masking on, with the specified Amplitude (the higher, the higher the effect, and the lower the quality).
  • "framerate": Number of frame-per-seconds to achieve when bit-rate control is on.
  • "quant": Specify the quantizer as a float value.
  • "base-quant": Specify the base quantizer as a float value.
  • "search-size": Specify the search-window size as a float value.
  • "base-search-size":Specify the base search-window size as a float value.

Note: call to Set_Param() can occur any time during the encoding process and will take effect immediately during next call to Encode(). In particular, this method can be used during two-pass encoding to restore modified parameters stored in a first-pass file.

Parameters:
Value the parameter value to set.

virtual int SKL_MP4_ANALYZER::Set_Param (  const char *const   Param,
int  Value
)  [pure virtual]
 

Sets the value of the integer parameter described by Param string. These parameter settings control the core of the codec during motion estimation and bitstream coding.

Returns:
0 if parameter is not applicable, or -1 if Value has invalid range.
Parameters:
Param can be one of the following constant strings:
  • "buffer-size": If non-zero, specify the size of coded bits buffer to allocate for each frame coding.

  • "quant": Sets the main quantizer (the lower, the higher the quality). Should be in range [1..31]. When variable bitrate is used, this value is internally adjusted after each frame coding, while trying to meet the desired bitrate.
  • "base-quant": Similar to "quant" parameter, but sets the quantizer of key-frames. By default, key-frames are assumed to share the global quantizer, if this parameter is not set.
  • "quant-type": Quantization method used: 0 for H263's, 1 for MPEG4's.

  • "intra-limit": Percentage of INTRA macroblock necessary to trigger a scene change (with key frame signaled).
  • "intra-max-delay": Maximum allowed number of P-frames between each INTRA key-frame.
  • "intra-max-count": Counter for "intra-max-delay". When it reaches 0, an INTRA frame is emitted.

  • "rounding": Block-interpolation rounding mode: 0 (down) or 1 (up)
  • "subpixel": Subpixel precision to use: 0=full-pixel, 1=half-pixel (default), 2=quarter pixel.
  • "use-trellis": Switch trellis-based quantization on (non-zero Value) or off (Value=0)

  • "search-size": size in 16-pixels units of the search window. When bitrate control is on, this size is changed adaptively.
  • "base-search-size": Similar to "search-size", this is the default value used as starting point after each key-frame.

  • "search-method": Internal searcho method (0 to 5)
  • "search-metric": Metric for evaluating match: 0=SAD (default), 1=SSD, 2=Hadamard.

  • "4v-probing": Worthiness Threshold (as percentage) of 4-motion-vector blocks to probe.

  • "sad-skip-limit": Metric threshold below which a block is SKIPed
  • "sad-intra-limit": Metric threshold above which a block is coded INTRA
  • "inter-threshold": Amplitude of INTER coefficient below which a sub-block is skipped (using Coded Block Pattern)

  • "bitrate": If non-zero, turns (simplistic) bitrate control on. It then will adjust "quant", "search-size" parameter, and use the "framerate" setting too.

  • "field-pred-probing": Worthiness Threshold (as percentage) for field/frame block decision.
  • "interlace-field": Use interlace-block coding if non-zero
  • "interlace-dct": Use interlace-block DCT coding of coefficient, if non-zero

  • "gmc-pts": Number of GMC points to use (0 to 3)
  • "gmc-mode": If non-zero, turns GMC estimation on (experimental)
  • "gmc-accuracy": GMC interpolation precision (0 to 3)

  • "reduced-frame": Use reduced-frame coding, if non-zero
  • "luminance-masking": Switch luminance-masking on (non-zero Value) or off (Value=0)

  • "frequency": Frequency of frame-tick resets.
  • "frame-ticks": Precision of frame-tick coding

Parameters:
Value the parameter value to set.
Note: call to Set_Param() can occur any time during the encoding process and will take effect immediately during next call to Encode(). In particular, this method can be used during two-pass encoding to restore modified parameters stored in a first-pass file.

virtual void SKL_MP4_ANALYZER::Shut_Down (   )  [pure virtual]
 

this function is called when the invoked analyzer is about to be disabled.

virtual void SKL_MP4_ANALYZER::Wake_Up (  SKL_MP4_ANALYZER *  Previous  )  [pure virtual]
 

This function is called when the invoked analyzer is about to be enabled.
